papers.title_en: Interception-Driven Inverse Reachability for Engagement Zone Construction
papers.authors: Grant Stagg, Cameron K. Peterson, Alexander Von Moll, Isaac Weintraub
papers.evidence: 基于拦截驱动的逆向可达性构建单无人机交战区域
papers.tldr: 针对对抗环境中追击者发射位置未知的问题，提出拦截驱动逆可达性框架。每次拦截事件对未知发射点施加几何约束，求交得包含真实位置的有界集，再映射至追击者可达区域构建确定性交战区域，提供最坏情况安全解释。概率扩展模型发射位置不确定性，生成分级交战风险场。引入信息驱动规划器引导牺牲性智能体选择轨迹，最大化期望收缩发射区域。蒙特卡洛模拟表明该框架快速降低位置不确定性，少量牺牲部署后安全轨迹显著缩短。
papers.abstract_en: In contested environments, autonomous vehicles may need to plan around adversarial pursuers whose launch locations are unknown. This paper presents an interception-driven inverse-reachability framework for inferring a feasible pursuer launch region directly from observed interception events for a single pursuer. Each interception induces a geometric constraint on the unknown launch location, and intersecting these constraints yields a bounded set guaranteed to contain the true origin under maximum-capability assumptions. Mapping this inferred set through the pursuer reachable region produces deterministic engagement zones with an explicit worst-case safety interpretation. A probabilistic extension models uncertainty in the pursuer launch location and yields graded engagement-risk fields for risk-aware planning. To accelerate localization, we introduce an information-driven planner for sacrificial agents that selects trajectories to maximize expected contraction of the feasible launch region. Monte Carlo simulations show that the proposed framework rapidly reduces launch-location uncertainty and enables substantially shorter safe trajectories after only a small number of sacrificial deployments.
